changes.before: Eastweald looked healthy in a way that felt insolent.
changes.after: Trimmed ditches and fresh labor kept Eastweald too neat for famine country.

changes.before: Enchanted stones warmed the walls. Imported work. Old money paid for it.
changes.after: Enchanted stones warmed the walls where a fire should have been.

changes.before: No ink stained his nails, and the thumb that rested on the chair arm bore no callus. Whatever labor his house required, none of it ran through those hands.
changes.after: No ink stained his nails, and the thumb on the chair arm bore no callus.

changes.before: That was the first break in him, small and brief enough that most men would have trusted it not to show.
changes.after: A flicker crossed his face before the composure settled back into place.

changes.before: They had expected scrutiny. They had not expected it from someone with enough rank to force answers.
changes.after: This was scrutiny backed by rank.

changes.before: He trusted her speed.
changes.after: Relief eased his shoulders the moment Jandice took over.

changes.before: Enough to warn her what the way out would cost.
changes.after: Enough to leave the ward's taste there.

changes.before: The assistants, ledgers, and open benches told her more than any hidden room could have.
changes.after: Open benches, spread ledgers, assistants bent over their copying.

changes.before: The archive took up a long chamber paneled in dark wood gone dry with age.
changes.after: Dry dark paneling boxed the archive around three lamp-weighted tables.

changes.before: Even so, the thought moving toward it ran in a lane she could follow without stumbling.
changes.after: Even so, the line of thought pointed at the same missing step she had seen in the farmhouse.

changes.before: The lower floors were everywhere in the ledger even when they went unnamed: references cut short, samples transferred inward, pressure readings taken beneath the archive foundation.
changes.after: Repeated references to inward transfers and pressure readings pointed below the archive.

changes.before: For the first time Jandice went still in a way that was not practiced.
changes.after: For the first time, Jandice went still and did not recover it at once.

changes.before: Those hands had done real work, and dangerous work, enough to pull Jaina farther in.
changes.after: Bruise-darkness and chalk cuts marked them as a working mage's hands.

changes.before: A preparation chamber held bodies on tables with linen over the face and numbered tags at the wrist.
changes.after: Bodies lay on numbered tables under linen in the preparation chamber.

changes.before: The neatness made it worse.
changes.after: Clean instruments and straightened tags were worse than rot would have been.

changes.before: They returned her at last to a guest chamber in an upper residential wing with windows over Darrowmere's black water.
changes.after: They returned her to an upper guest chamber overlooking Darrowmere's black water.

changes.before: The sending stone in her satchel felt heavier for being thought about.
changes.after: The sending stone in her satchel grew heavier the moment she considered it.

changes.before: The line fought her all the way.
changes.after: Distance and ward pressure roughened the signal all the way back to Dalaran.

changes.before: Jandice stepped to the desk, bent over the notes, and forgot to perform for a few dangerous seconds.
changes.after: Jandice bent over the notes so fast the smile left her face for a few seconds.

changes.before: For a heartbeat the room sharpened to a knife edge between them.
changes.after: For a heartbeat, neither of them moved.

changes.before: For the first time all day her poise slipped.
changes.after: Jandice held her gaze long enough for the polish to slip.

changes.before: The decision was out of her hands now.
changes.after: The master below had already decided.

changes.before: She had carried it out of the guest room and down the hidden stair on instinct, the way a soldier carried the map that might keep her alive two turns from now.
changes.after: She had carried it out of the guest room and down the hidden stair on instinct, like a mage refusing to lose the only proof in hand.
